items.park_reason: cross_jump-merge floor: GPU command wrapper with 2-way early-return -1 guards. Honest pure-C distance plateaus at 21 (target 49 insns, sandbox 47) across 7 structural variants: HEAD form, single-return goto-end, mixed inline-return+goto, do-while-0 wrapper, short-circuit &&, inlined dispatch (no fn/p locals), reordered packing. GCC jump2 cross_jump folds the two return -1 paths into a single epilogue tail; target keeps them split as beqz->end(li v0,-1 delay) + bnez->body(sll s1,16 delay) + j end(li v0,-1 delay). Sibling siblings gpu_LoadImage/gpu_StoreImage (no early-return guards) match in pure C; gpu_LoadImage/gpu_StoreImage prove the dispatch shape is matchable. The 19-rule cluster (incl 16-element reorder@21-36 + cascading register-rename substs /////) recreates targets scheduling/allocation cascading from the unmerged early-return shape - same structural family as documented saEft00Add (cross-jump-store-tail-merge). Needs user policy: either project-wide rodata reorder, canonical-asm authorization for the dispatch wrapper variant, or PERM_-directed permuter time budget

Register-rotation plateau (7 honest pure-C diffs, all reg-rename + 1 reorder in the final-block: target uses mask in , table in , return staged early in ; mine uses mask in , table in , store via ). 32 structural variants tested (return-stage alias, p/fn extract, comma operator, operand-flip, temp val, mask local, two-step assign, compound assign, volatile cast, array index, inverse mask, decomposed shifts, n-arg in tail, addr subtract, etc.) — all plateau at distance 7 with identical register-rotation diff structure. Sibling gpu_ClearOTag at the same file uses the same C pattern  and compiles to MINE pattern (mask in v1, table in v0), proving the GCC-natural codegen for this expression is mine. Target compiled from a different C structure I have not found in 32 variants. Same character as documented [[register-alloc-pure-c]] cpu_side_move_dir_4 plateau but smaller (7 vs 15). Remaining avenues: directed permuter from clean single-fn target (untried, requires 30+ min setup + run); instrumented cc1 alloc dump.

ASM-SUSPECT/structural — 298-insn gpu_PutDispEnv-style display environment setup (cached-diff + GP1 packet sends via g_gpu_dev_table[0x10]). Asmfix rule replace_with_asmfile bridges. Partial pure-C reconstruction from m2c brings sandbox 296 -> 132 (286/298 insns). Remaining 132-distance gap: 6 missing lhu+sll/sra cast-pattern pairs (GCC combine folds (s32)(s16)u16var to lh; needs structural lever to defeat fold), plus scheduling/register-allocation cascade through the 4-comparison + video-mode + GP1 packet build region. Multi-session pure-C work; needs same depth as defeat-licm-hoist-var-reuse class fixes. Oracle SHA1 green throughout (asmfix continues to provide target bytes).

Pure-C floor at sandbox-distance 4. Single 4-insn prologue reorder rule (regfix.txt:3753) flips save+def pairing from cc1's natural [sw s1; move s1,a0; sw s0; move s0,a1] to target's [sw s0; move s0,a1; sw s1; move s1,a0]. Mechanism: cc1's mips.c save_restore_insns iterates GP_REG_LAST->FIRST (3000a) and expand_function_start creates pseudos in DECL_ARGUMENTS order (out=72->s1, r=73->s0). Save-order is structurally fixed by mips machdep + ABI param order. Tried 9 distinct C source levers: early r-temps (3 forms), local alias, K&R-style decl, K&R-with-reversed-decl-order, var_a3=7 init, 3rd-callee-save force, all-temp-stores-at-end - all either CSE'd back or regressed. Sibling func_8007C4B8 has identical issue (twin function, same rule shape). Per [[no-compiler-divergence]] mips.c is frozen; per ABI param swap would break the asm callers. Either accept prologue_config.json as infrastructure (policy decision) or authorize ASM-region for the prologue-only debt.

prologue save-order structural — twin of just-parked func_8007C2A0 (2b3ccfe). Single 4-insn regfix reorder 3,4,1,2 @ 1-4 swaps cc1's natural [sw s1,52; move s1,a0; sw s0,48; move s0,a1] to target's [sw s0,48; move s0,a1; sw s1,52; move s1,a0]. Same identical mips.c save_restore_insns iteration-order wall, same identical C signature (s32 *out, Rect *r), same identical body shape. Honest sandbox=4 verified fixed across the 9 levers tested on the sibling + 1 additional probe (s16 rx,ry locals + tmp_v0 call-result temp) this session: same 4-distance floor. Path to COMPLETED-C identical to sibling: requires either function-scope canonical-asm wrapping of the prologue 4-insn block, treating regfix reorder as canonical infrastructure (like prologue_config.json's AddTbpOfst_80047EE8 family), or a novel C lever spanning the mips.c scheduling space the explored levers don't cover.

register-rotation cluster (21 regfix subst + 1 manufactured insert at idx 15 = lost-codegen-insert move a3,v0); pure-C levers tested (A block-local split, B s32 type, structural restructuring of clamp/dispatch, dispatch-test type changes, var_a1 init reordering) all neutral or regressive. Best honest score 18 (50 vs 51 insns; loses the manufactured save). Same wall pattern as cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec confirmed limits in [[register-alloc-pure-c]] — register-rotation cluster cc1's allocator doesn't naturally produce; needs directed permuter sweep from clean target (per [[difficult-is-not-impossible]] § resume avenues). Sibling func_8007C86C has identical structure + same cheat shape. Not a jtbl-infra or GTE-wrapper auto-confirm; novel pure-C-resistant cluster needing user judgment on permuter budget vs parking until next iteration.

RA-coupling plateau (same cluster as sibling func_8007C7A0): 21 rules including 19 pure register-rename substs (target uses // where cc1 picks // via natural ascending preference) + 1 frame-size subst (cc1: 24-byte frame; target: 16-byte) + 1 lost-codegen delete+insert manufacturing 'addu ,,' at idx 15. Honest pure-C distance 20, structural 51/51 insns match. Tried 5+ C restructurings (s32 args + s16 casts, arg0_orig preserve, reduced locals, var split, ternary-style chain) — all regressed or flat. Pattern matches cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec RA-coupling family (sessions 5-10 evidence in register-alloc-pure-c.md). Sibling func_8007C7A0 has IDENTICAL 21-rule cluster — not a one-off. Needs canonical-asm authorization decision or directed permuter.

register-allocation gap: 2 cheat-asm pins (var_v0->, var_v1->) carry the match. Honest pure-C distance 5 (4 with new_var2 const chained-OR sibling-shape improvement). Target allocates var_v0 (arg0 & MASK) to ; GCC naturally chooses  (reusing dead arg0 reg). Sibling siblings (func_8007C7A0/8007C86C) reach  because their var_v0_2 lives across a bounds-check pre-stage in a callee-save before the andi; func_8007C938 has no such pre-stage. Tried: sibling-style new_var2 = var_v0_2 chain (regresses to 7), pre-init var_v0 = arg0 (no effect, DCE), factored mask outside if (+1 insn, score 10), in-place |= form (regresses to 5), inline literal (regresses to 5), s16/u16 arg types (no effect, ABI promotion), OR operand swap (commutative, no effect), gtype local (no effect, folded), intermediate new_var local (no effect, DCE). The 2 pins are documented register-asm-pins per .claude/rules/register-asm-pins.md; pure-C levers from .claude/rules/register-alloc-pure-c.md (Levers A/B/C; D forbidden) do not have a target here because there is no shared variable to split (Lever A), narrow type has no effect (Lever B), no loop (Lever C).

cross-jump merge DIRECTION wall: GCC merges case 1 and case 2 tails into a shared .L139 mini-block (j; subu(delay)) instead of target's layout where case 2 back-jumps into case 1's body (3-insn merge inline). Tried 12+ pure-C structures from sandbox-13 baseline; best is sandbox-11 via v0_tmp temp split (which fixes the lh load order swap that was issue A). Residual 11 reflects 2 coupled divergences: (1) cross-jump direction (.L139 mini-block vs back-jump-to-case-1-subu); (2) case 2 BE77==0 path register allocation (lhu \ vs target lhu \). Original match commit d1dff7c explicitly acknowledged this as 'matching target tail-merge layout instead of GCC case-2-shared' requiring 11 regfix rules. NOT a documented auto-park category. ESCALATE: structural cross-jump merge direction wall in switch dispatch — same kind as cross-jump-store-tail-merge but for tail-merge DIRECTION (which block keeps the merged code) rather than defeating merge. Resume avenues: directed permuter from clean target.o (per difficult-is-not-impossible); investigation of jump.c find_cross_jump suffix selection heuristics; potential rule update if pattern recurs.

ASM-SUSPECT: distance 149 (151-insn GPU DMA list builder — clamp y/h into rect, build 13-15 element DMA packet at D_800F1858, call gpu_GetInfo x3, gpu_StartDmaList). Bounded pure-C attempt this session: 149 -> 76 (correct structural decomp, signature, branches, big-vs-small-packet paths, all packet assignments). Residual 76 is register-allocation (target arg0 in t0, mine in a2) + clamp-block control-flow (target uses shared-end-label sh-once pattern with delay-slot moves; my if-else stores in both arms) + big-packet scheduling. Distance is large enough to need a dedicated full session; out of scope for iter 15/15. Per engine ASM-SUSPECT routing 'bounded attempt then PARK'. Resume avenues: (1) restructure clamp as 3-way ternary with shared sh at end-label, (2) figure out arg0=t0 alloc (likely from arg liveness shape — arg0 is read on entry but goes dead, mid-function reads arg0[]/arg0[2]), (3) big-packet scheduling depends on the order of D_800F18xx assignments.

Semantic frame investigation (memory/project/func-8007ce0c-semantic-investigation.md, 2026-06-01): 32-byte vars region (0x10-0x2F) in target frame=80 is entirely UNREFERENCED in func body — confirmed by reading every -relative access in asm/funcs/func_8007CE0C.s. cc1 emits frame=64 from any tested pure-C source; only forbidden patterns (s32 buf[N], (void)&local — both detected by engine/volatile_cheats) reproduce target frame size. NOT canonical-asm: scan_hand_coded tier=LOW score=1/8 (only S4); diagnose's CANONICAL-ASM verdict is false positive (mflo heuristic over-fires). Pure-C floor 41 reachable via compare-flip + drop cheat-asm pin (vs HEAD 48), but no rule retirement so not committed. Per [[no-new-park-categories]] no frame-size-infrastructure carve-out; per [[dead-vars-local-array]] no padding workaround. 23 rules remain (now drift-robust via {lbl#N} per db690e7, so this function is no longer a sibling-coupling blocker). REVISIT WHEN: new C lever discovered (e.g. via instrumented cc1 alloc/sched dumps in the BB2_*_DEBUG family), OR hand-coded-signal reassessment reveals evidence the scan_hand_coded heuristic missed.

Canonical jump-table infrastructure, not a register/codegen cheat. The 24 asmfix rules (15 renames of GCC .L labels to external jtbl_800108CC + 8 jlabel conversions + 1 delete_between stripping GCC's emitted table) are structurally required: the switch's jump table jtbl_800108CC sits in the FIRST rodata block (asm/data 800.rodata_pre.o, bb2.ld:25, addr 0x800108CC), while code6cac_b2.o's own .rodata links far later (bb2.ld:50). A GCC in-object jump table can only land in code6cac_b2's late rodata, never at 0x108CC, so referencing the external jtbl symbol + deleting GCC's table is mandatory. Zero-asmfix COMPLETED-C would need relocating the jtbl into code6cac_b2.c AND reordering the whole global rodata layout (project-wide, needs user sign-off). This is the project standard for the 26 jtbl-rename functions whose tables splat carved into asm/data rodata. Separately, sandbox cannot score this file: file-wide cheat-asm stripping shifts maspsx indices and crashes a sibling fn's index-based regfix reorder rule (KeyError in tools/regfix.py).

register-alloc coupling — arg pseudos win allocno tiebreaker for low callee-saves (creation order), forcing rotation. chain-extender already present, volatile-bump regresses (cost > saved). same mechanism as cpu_side_move_dir_4 at larger surface; full pinning in register-alloc-pure-c.md.
